perturbation.original_text: The term "Confidential Information" shall mean any information and data of a confidential nature belonging to the disclosing party, its affiliates and/or licensors ("Discloser"), including without limitation, proprietary, technical, developmental, marketing, sales, operating, financial, performance, cost, business and process information and plans, software, and computer programming techniques which are disclosed or made available pursuant to this Agreement in connection with the Evaluation.
perturbation.changed_text: The term "Confidential Information" shall mean information and data disclosed by the disclosing party ("Discloser") under this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The modified definition of "Confidential Information" is overly broad and lacks specific examples like proprietary, technical, or financial data, which are included in the original definition. This weakens the definition and creates a contradiction because subsequent clauses assume a more comprehensive definition. By omitting key elements of what constitutes "Confidential Information," the scope of protection becomes ambiguous, and the enforceability of the confidentiality obligations is uncertain. This creates a contradiction because later sections detailing permitted use, return obligations, and remedies rely on a clear and comprehensive definition of Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon termination of the Evaluation of the parties' relationship with respect thereto, and upon Discloser's request, Recipient shall return to Discloser all materials reflecting or containing any of Discloser's Confidential Information and shall not retain any copies, extracts or other reproductions in whole or in part of any of the foregoing.
perturbation.justification: The original text provides a comprehensive definition of "Confidential Information," including specific examples like proprietary, technical, and financial data. The changed text significantly narrows this definition to simply "information and data disclosed by the disclosing party." This creates a contradiction with the contradicted text, which assumes a more comprehensive definition of "Confidential Information" when specifying the return of "all materials reflecting or containing any of Discloser's Confidential Information." The narrowed definition in the changed text makes it unclear what materials must be returned, leading to potential disputes and rendering the return obligation ambiguous and less enforceable. This aligns with the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient hereby agrees that the Confidential Information will be used by it solely for the purposes of discussions with Discloser relating to the Evaluation and as necessary to fulfill any obligations Recipient may have to Discloser pursuant to any agreements the parties may enter into or otherwise. Recipient agrees not to disclose the Confidential Information of the other party, in any form, to any third party except as contemplated herein. Recipient agrees to maintain the confidential nature of the Confidential Information; provided however, that any such Confidential Information may be disclosed to its employees who need access to such <PAGE> information for such purposes and are made aware of and agree to be bound by the confidentiality obligations contained herein.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient hereby agrees that the Confidential Information will be used by it for discussions with Discloser relating to the Evaluation. Recipient agrees not to disclose the Confidential Information of the other party, in any form, to any third party. Recipient agrees to maintain the confidential nature of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "and as necessary to fulfill any obligations Recipient may have to Discloser pursuant to any agreements the parties may enter into or otherwise", the contract now strictly limits the use of confidential information. The original text allows for uses beyond mere discussions, recognizing that obligations from other agreements might necessitate using the information. By omitting this, a contradiction is created because the recipient is now restricted to 'discussions' only, regardless of pre-existing agreements that might require broader use of the information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient hereby agrees that the Confidential Information will be used by it for discussions with Discloser relating to the Evaluation. Recipient agrees not to disclose the Confidential Information of the other party, in any form, to any third party. Recipient agrees to maintain the confidential nature of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text allows the recipient to use confidential information not only for discussions related to the evaluation but also to fulfill obligations under other agreements. The changed text removes the allowance for using confidential information to fulfill obligations under other agreements, restricting its use solely to 'discussions'. This directly contradicts the implied understanding that confidential information could be used for broader purposes stemming from other agreements between the parties as it was initially constructed, and the new, more limited use directly creates contradiction within the document, specifically in the contradicted text, which is in the same location as the changed text.

perturbation.original_text: Except for the software products and GO data, if any, disclosed hereunder, Confidential Information shall not include information which (a) is known to the party receiving the information from Discloser ("Recipient") at the time of disclosure and is not subject to restriction; (b) is now or subsequently becomes generally known or available to the Recipient by publication, commercial use or otherwise through no fault of Recipient; (c) is lawfully obtained from a third party who has the right to make such disclosure; or (d) is independently developed by or for the Recipient without access to the Discloser's Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information shall not include information which (a) is known to the party receiving the information from Discloser ("Recipient") at the time of disclosure and is not subject to restriction; (b) is now or subsequently becomes generally known or available to the Recipient by publication, commercial use or otherwise through no fault of Recipient; (c) is lawfully obtained from a third party who has the right to make such disclosure; or (d) is independently developed by or for the Recipient without access to the Discloser's Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'Except for the software products and GO data, if any, disclosed hereunder,' the clause now implies that ANY information, including software products and GO data, may fall under the exceptions of Confidential Information. This directly contradicts the original intent, which explicitly sought to include software products and GO data as Confidential Information by default. This omission creates a contradiction because other sections of the agreement may rely on software and GO data being treated as confidential, but this clause now opens the door for them to be excluded.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information shall not include information which (a) is known to the party receiving the information from Discloser ("Recipient") at the time of disclosure and is not subject to restriction; (b) is now or subsequently becomes generally known or available to the Recipient by publication, commercial use or otherwise through no fault of Recipient; (c) is lawfully obtained from a third party who has the right to make such disclosure; or (d) is independently developed by or for the Recipient without access to the Discloser's Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits 'Except for the software products and GO data, if any, disclosed hereunder,' which was present in the original text. According to the perturbation explanation, this creates a contradiction because the clause now implies that ANY information, including software products and GO data, may fall under the exceptions of Confidential Information. This directly contradicts the original intent, which explicitly sought to include software products and GO data as Confidential Information by default. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, meaning it also does not include the clause that software products and GO data are to be considered confidential. Since the changed text allows for software products and GO data to potentially be excluded from confidential information, while the original intent and other sections of the contract may require this data to be confidential, there is an in-text contradiction as stated in the problem description, based on the Omissions - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: The parties hereto shall not be obligated to compensate each other for disclosure of any information under this Agreement and agree that no warranties of any kind are given with respect to such information, as well as any use thereof. It is understood that no patent, copyright, trademark or other proprietary right or license is granted by this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties hereto shall not be obligated to compensate each other for disclosure of any information under this Agreement and agree that no warranties of any kind are given with respect to such information, as well as any use thereof.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'It is understood that no patent, copyright, trademark or other proprietary right or license is granted by this Agreement', the contract omits a key disclaimer about intellectual property rights. The original clause explicitly states that the agreement doesn't grant any license or right to patents, copyrights, or trademarks. The omission now creates uncertainty about the implications regarding intellectual property. This can be read to mean that the agreement does grant a right, impliedly. The absence creates an ambiguity and the remaining text can be interpreted to assume that no licenses are granted, which contradicts the new state of affairs as licenses can be granted implicitly.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties hereto shall not be obligated to compensate each other for disclosure of any information under this Agreement and agree that no warranties of any kind are given with respect to such information, as well as any use thereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly disclaims the granting of any patent, copyright, trademark, or other proprietary right or license. The changed text omits this disclaimer, creating ambiguity. While the contradicted text states 'no warranties of any kind are given with respect to such information, as well as any use thereof', the changed text's omission introduces uncertainty regarding intellectual property rights. Without the disclaimer, it can be interpreted that licenses or proprietary rights could be implicitly granted, contradicting the implied stance of the remaining text.
